======= RMA03786Juris Hartmanis papers16-13-3786Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

physcThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:12:04 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Juris Hartmanis papers
Hartmanis, Juris
16-13-3786
21 cubic feet. 21 cubic feet.
1965-2008. Correspondence, Publications, Research Materials Includes publications by Hartmanis and others in the field of computer science, correspondence, and subject files. Collection material in English 6788412
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Daniel W. Young
Date completed:
August 2010
EAD encoding:
Daniel W. Young, March 2010, August 2010
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Emeritus professor of Computer Science, Cornell University.
Hartmanis was born in Latvia, received his Bachelor's degree from the University of Marburg in Germany, his Master's degree in Applied Mathematics from the University of Kansas City in 1951, and his Ph.D. in Mathematics from the California Institute of Technology in 1955. After teaching at Cornell and Ohio State, he joined the General Electric Research Laboratory in 1958, where he developed principles of computational complexity theory. In 1965 he became a professor at Cornell and was on of the founders and first chairman of its computer science department. In 1993, he won the Turing Award along with Richard Stearns.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes publications by Hartmanis and others in the field of computer science, correspondence, and subject files.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Papers by Various Computer Scientists Boxes 1-7; Box 8- Folders: 1-59
Series II. Reprints - Hartmanis Box 9- Folders: 1-66
Series III. Course Material Box 9- Folders: 66-88; Box 10- Folders: 1-9
Series IV. Correspondence Box 10- Folders: 10-20, 27-37; Box 11- Folder: 10
Series V. Lecture and Subject Files Box 10- Folders: 38-44; Box 11- Folders: 7-9, 11-50
Series VI. Department of Computer Science Material Box 10- Folders: 21-26; Box 21
Series VII. National Science Foundation (NSF) Grant Material Box 10- Folders: 45-47; Box 11- Folders: 1-6, 43, 47; Box 18
Series VIII. Other Professional Material Boxes 11-20
General
Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/14935
Cite As:
Juris Hartmanis papers, #16-13-3786. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Computational complexity Machine theory Computer science Cornell University -- : Faculty. College of Engineering. Department of Computer Science
